solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def minChanges(self, n: int, k: int) -> int:
        if (n & k) != k:
            return -1
        changes = 0
        while n or k:
            n_bit = n & 1
            k_bit = k & 1
            if n_bit > k_bit:
                changes += 1
            n >>= 1
            k >>= 1
        return changes
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution works by first checking if it's possible to make n equal to k by verifying if all 1-bits in k are also 1-bits in n. Then, it iterates through the bits of n and k from right to left, counting the number of changes needed to make n equal to k. The bitwise operations ensure an efficient solution.
